meta.title: Endocrinology Practice Software | Diabetes RTM & CGM Integration | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to endocrinology practice operations. $11,700/month in diabetes RTM. CGM data integration. Thyroid nodule tracking. Insulin titration management.

hero.subhead: Your diabetes patients already track glucose — that's billable RTM. Your CGM data needs integration into workflows. Your thyroid nodules need tracking. clinIQ captures diabetes RTM revenue, integrates CGM data, and manages chronic endocrine conditions.

sections.content:
Endocrinology practices manage chronic conditions that require ongoing monitoring and frequent adjustments.

**The diabetes opportunity:**

Diabetes management happens between visits. Glucose monitoring, medication compliance, diet and exercise, hypoglycemia events — all occur at home. This data informs treatment.

Most endocrinology practices don't systematically capture this data — and they're not billing for the monitoring they do.

90 diabetes patients × $130/month = $11,700/month = $140,400/year in RTM.

**The CGM data challenge:**

Continuous glucose monitors generate mountains of data. Time in range, glucose variability, hypoglycemia events, average glucose. The data exists, but integrating it into clinical workflows is cumbersome.

Patients bring printouts. Staff manually reviews Clarity or LibreView. Data doesn't flow into the chart automatically.

**The thyroid nodule tracking problem:**

Endocrinologists follow hundreds of thyroid nodules. Each needs periodic imaging based on size and characteristics. Without tracking, follow-up imaging falls through cracks. Cancers get missed.

**The insulin titration burden:**

New insulin patients need frequent dose adjustments. Call-back protocols are time-consuming and poorly documented. Remote titration should be systematic and billable.

Diabetes is ideally suited for RTM. Patients already track glucose (or should). Treatment changes based on that data. RTM makes this monitoring billable.

**What diabetes patients track:**

Glucose values:
- Fingerstick logs (for non-CGM patients)
- CGM summary data (time in range, average, variability)
- Hypoglycemia events (frequency, severity, timing)

Medication compliance:
- Insulin timing and doses
- Oral medication compliance
- GLP-1 injection compliance

Lifestyle factors:
- Diet patterns
- Exercise frequency
- Sick days

Symptoms:
- Hypoglycemia symptoms
- Hyperglycemia symptoms
- Neuropathy, vision changes, other complications

**The CPT codes:**

98975: RTM setup — one-time. ~$20-25.
98977: RTM device supply. ~$50-60/month.
98980: RTM treatment management — first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.
98981: Additional 20-minute increments.

Typical: $120-150/patient/month.

**RTM vs RPM for diabetes:**

RPM (99453-99458) requires physiological devices transmitting data. CGM data can qualify.

RTM (98975-98981) covers patient-reported data — symptoms, compliance, non-device glucose logs.

Many diabetes patients qualify for both. clinIQ tracks which codes apply to which patients.

**What clinIQ does for diabetes RTM:**

Glucose logging: For non-CGM patients, simple app-based glucose logging.

CGM integration: CGM data pulled into dashboard (see next section).

Hypoglycemia tracking: Frequency, severity, and patterns visible.

Medication compliance: Insulin and oral med compliance tracked.

Alert thresholds: Frequent hypoglycemia? Time in range declining? A1c prediction concerning? Alerts trigger.

CGM usage is exploding. Dexcom, Libre, Medtronic, Eversense — patients are generating continuous glucose data. Using it efficiently is the challenge.

**The current workflow:**

Patient arrives. Someone logs into Clarity or LibreView. Downloads report. Prints or uploads to chart. Endocrinologist reviews. Treatment decision made.

This takes 5-10 minutes per patient. With 20 diabetes visits/day, that's 2+ hours of manual data work.

**What clinIQ does:**

CGM dashboard integration: Dexcom Clarity, Abbott LibreView data pulled into clinIQ dashboard.

Pre-visit summary: Before patient arrives, CGM summary visible — time in range, average glucose, GMI, hypoglycemia events.

Trend visualization: 14-day, 30-day, 90-day trends without logging into vendor portals.

Alert-based review: Patients with concerning patterns flagged. Stable patients don't require deep review.

**The time savings:**

5 minutes saved per patient × 20 patients/day = 100 minutes/day = 8+ hours/week.

That time can be redirected to patient care or used to see more patients.

**RTM billing with CGM:**

CGM data review contributes to RTM time. Reviewing CGM data, making treatment decisions, communicating with patients — all documented toward the 20-minute threshold.

New insulin patients need frequent dose adjustments. The traditional approach: call back in a week, report your fasting glucoses, we'll adjust the dose.

This is time-consuming, poorly documented, and often not billed.

**The titration workflow:**

Patient starts basal insulin. Checks fasting glucose daily. Reports back weekly. Dose adjusted based on average fasting glucose. Repeat until at goal.

Without systems, this relies on:
- Patients calling back (many don't)
- Staff documenting phone conversations
- Providers making decisions from incomplete data

**What clinIQ does for insulin titration:**

Patient app for glucose logging: Daily fasting glucose entered into app.

Titration protocol visibility: Current dose, target range, actual values, recommended adjustment.

Provider-assisted titration: Provider sees data, makes decision, communicates through app.

Documentation: Every titration interaction documented automatically.

**Billing for titration:**

Insulin titration work can be billed as:
- RTM treatment management (98980) if patient is enrolled in RTM
- Telephone E/M (99441-99443) for significant phone calls
- E/M if done during office visit

clinIQ ensures the work is documented appropriately for billing.

Endocrinologists follow hundreds of thyroid nodules. Each has:
- Size and characteristics
- Bethesda category (if biopsied)
- Recommended follow-up interval
- Last imaging date
- Next imaging due

**The tracking problem:**

Patient had thyroid US 2 years ago showing 1.2cm nodule. Follow-up recommended in 12 months. It's now 24 months. Did anyone order the follow-up US? Has it been done? Were results reviewed?

Often, nobody knows. Nodules are lost to follow-up. Some of those nodules are cancer.

**What clinIQ does for thyroid tracking:**

Nodule registry: Every patient with thyroid nodules, nodule characteristics, follow-up interval, imaging status.

Follow-up due alerts: 'US due in 30 days.' 'US overdue by 3 months.'

Imaging pipeline: US ordered → scheduled → completed → read → patient informed.

Biopsy tracking: If biopsy performed, Bethesda result, follow-up based on cytology.

**The liability protection:**

Missed thyroid cancer is a malpractice risk. Systematic nodule tracking with documented follow-up creates defensible care and better outcomes.

Endocrinology visits vary:

- New patient diabetes: 45-60 minutes
- Diabetes follow-up: 20-30 minutes
- Thyroid follow-up: 15-20 minutes
- Complex multi-condition: 40-60 minutes
- Insulin start: 45-60 minutes (education included)
- CGM start: 30-45 minutes

**What clinIQ does for patient flow:**

Pre-visit data: CGM summary, recent glucose logs, A1c, medication list — visible before patient arrives.

Visit type awareness: Different visit types have different expected durations and data needs.

Education tracking: Diabetes education, CGM training — documented with completion status.

Queue visibility: Who's waiting, who's ready, who needs pre-visit data review.

Endocrinology implementation focuses on diabetes RTM, CGM integration, and thyroid tracking.

**Week 1: Configuration**

We configure for your practice: visit types, CGM integration, RTM templates, thyroid tracking.

CGM integration: Dexcom Clarity and/or LibreView connection established.

Thyroid registry: Existing nodule patients loaded with imaging history.

**Week 2: Training**

Clinical staff learns CGM dashboard. Providers learn RTM workflow. Coordinators learn thyroid tracking.

**Week 3: Go-Live**

RTM enrollment beginning. CGM integration live. Thyroid tracking active.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: CGM workflow improvement immediate. RTM enrollment starting.

Month 3: RTM billing at scale (70-90 patients). Thyroid tracking preventing lost nodules.

Month 6: Full RTM revenue ($10,000-12,000/month). CGM workflow optimized.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 90 patients × $130/month = $11,700/month = $140,400/year

CGM workflow savings: 8 hours/week staff time = $20,000+/year in labor

Thyroid tracking: Risk mitigation (one prevented missed cancer = significant liability avoided)

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

CGM integration: Included (Dexcom/Abbott)

faqs.a: We connect to Dexcom Clarity and Abbott LibreView. Patient data flows into clinIQ dashboard. Pre-visit summary shows time in range, average glucose, hypoglycemia events without logging into vendor portals.

faqs.q: Is diabetes RTM different from RPM?
faqs.a: RTM covers patient-reported data (symptoms, compliance, manual glucose logs). RPM covers device-transmitted physiological data (CGM can qualify). Many patients may be eligible for both programs.

faqs.q: How do you track insulin titration?
faqs.a: Patient logs glucose in app. Titration protocol shows current dose, target, actual values. Provider makes decision, communicates through app. All documented for billing.
Nodule registry with characteristics, follow-up intervals, imaging status. Alerts when follow-up is due or overdue. Complete imaging pipeline tracking.

faqs.q: Can we track other endocrine conditions?
faqs.a: Yes. Osteoporosis (DEXA tracking, medication compliance), adrenal disorders, pituitary conditions, PCOS — each can be configured with appropriate monitoring.
Insulin pump data integration varies by manufacturer. We can integrate with major platforms where APIs are available.

faqs.q: How does this work for CDEs/diabetes educators?
faqs.a: Diabetes educators can see patient data, document education visits, track education completion. Education time can contribute to billing.
Auth tracking for GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Mounjaro, etc.) — status, expiration, step therapy documentation.

testimonial.quote: We were manually pulling CGM data for every patient. Now it's there when they walk in. And the RTM revenue pays for multiple staff.
